mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and encouraging investment, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as market participants price in continued economic support.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains low but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative over a 1-year period, or if the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns turns significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits. This negative outlook drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 60-day horizon as investors anticipate an economic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by the specified trigger but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with low inflation indicates robust consumer demand without overheating, suggesting healthy economic expansion. This environment supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: If the specified conditions for RSAFS and CPIAUCSL are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during an economic expansion signals weakening manufacturing output and potential supply chain issues, which can dampen corporate profits. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for two consecutive months during an expansionary period, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in housing starts indicates a slowdown in construction activity and broader economic demand, often preceding a general economic downturn. This negative signal translates to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: If HOUST drops below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a strong labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 150,000, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and can lead to tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting corporate valuations. This results in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annualized 1-month change exceeds 4%, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and investor confidence. This positive economic backdrop drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows consistent growth (e.g., > 2% annualized) but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative over any 2-year period.
